3.happening: Building the dp table: every amount from 0 to {amount} starts at the infinity sentinel {amount + 1}.
3.why: A sentinel larger than any real answer means 'not reachable yet'.
3.invariant: dp[i] always holds the fewest coins known to make amount i.

4.happening: Base case: dp[0] = 0 — zero coins are needed to make amount 0.
4.why: Making nothing needs no coins; every other amount builds on this.

5.why: Amounts are solved smallest first, so smaller amounts are ready when larger ones need them.
5.invariant: Every amount below a already holds its fewest-coins count.

5.happening: No amounts to solve — the target is 0.
5.why: The base case dp[0] already holds the answer.
5.invariant: dp[0] is the answer when the target is 0.

6.why: Any coin no larger than a could be the last coin used to reach it.
6.invariant: We keep the fewest coins across every coin choice.

7.happening: Coin {coin} fits inside amount {a} — consider using it.
7.why: A coin larger than the amount cannot be the last coin.
7.invariant: Only coins no larger than a are candidates.

7.happening: Coin {coin} is larger than amount {a} — skip it.
7.why: It cannot be used to make this smaller amount.
7.invariant: Only coins no larger than a are candidates.

8.happening: Using coin {coin} gives {dp[a - coin] + 1} coins, beating the current {dp[a]}.
8.why: Fewer coins for this amount is an improvement worth recording.
8.invariant: dp[a] shrinks toward the true minimum.

8.happening: Using coin {coin} gives {dp[a - coin] + 1} coins, not better than {dp[a]}.
8.why: The existing count is already at least as good.

9.happening: Recording the improved count: dp[{a}] becomes {dp[a - coin] + 1}.
9.why: This coin choice reaches amount a with fewer coins than before.
9.invariant: dp[a] holds the fewest coins found so far.

10.happening: dp[{amount}] is still the sentinel — the amount cannot be made.
10.why: No coin combination ever reached the target.
10.invariant: An unreachable amount stays at the sentinel.

10.happening: dp[{amount}] holds a real count — the amount is reachable.
10.why: Some coin combination reached the target.
10.invariant: dp[amount] is the fewest coins for the target.

11.happening: Setting the answer to -1 — the amount is impossible to make.
11.why: The target was never reached by any coins.

13.why: The table's target slot holds the fewest coins.
13.invariant: dp[amount] is the minimum coin count.

14.happening: Returning {result} — the fewest coins to make {amount}.
14.why: The table is complete, so the count is final.
14.invariant: The answer is the minimum coins, or -1 if impossible.

init.happening: Setting up the table with a sentinel and the zero base case.
init.why: The recurrence needs a starting point and an 'infinity' for unreachable amounts.
init.invariant: dp[i] means the fewest coins to make amount i.

loop.happening: Sweeping amounts and trying each coin.
loop.why: Every amount is built from smaller amounts already solved.
loop.invariant: Amounts below the pointer are final.

check.happening: Testing whether a coin fits and whether it improves the count.
check.invariant: dp[a] holds the best count seen so far.

update.happening: Recording a smaller coin count for this amount.
update.why: A better coin choice was found.
update.invariant: dp[a] moves toward its true minimum.

return.invariant: dp[amount] is the fewest coins, or -1 if unreachable.
